LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT THE HAROLD GORDON NELSON YEAR 2002 REVOCABLE TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT BAR THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT UNCERTAIN. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS NOT NECESSARILY TIME-BARRED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S ALLEGATIONS REGARDING DELAYED DISCOVERY ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT BREACHED HIS DUTY OF CARE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ramsey F. Kawar, Esq., of Low, Ball & Lynch, Tel. 415.981.6630, appearing for defendant and moving party.

No other appearance. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NELSON, HAROLD GORDON, IND. AND AS TRUSTEE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the fraud claim and overruled as to all claims. The demurrer to the fraud claim is not opposed. The other claims that are challenged by the demurrer are adequately pled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not Reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ramsey F. Kawar, Esq., of Low, Ball & Lynch, Tel. 415.981.6630, appearing for defendant and moving party.

No other appearance. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE HAROLD GORDON NELSON YEAR 2002 REVOCABLE TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The answer to the complaint does not bar the cross-complaint. The breach of contract cause of action is not uncertain. The cross-complaint is not necessarily time-barred. Cross-complainant's allegations regarding delayed discovery are adequately pled. Cross-complainant sufficiently alleged that cross-defendant breached his duty of care. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Not Reported.
